---
title: "From Zero to Website with Quarto"
author: Charlotte Wickham
subtitle: Cascadia R Conf 2026
brand:
  color:
    palette: 
      blue: "#186897"
      orange: "#b2672d"
      light-grey: "#eee"
    primary: blue
  typography: 
    fonts: 
      - family: Crimson Text
        source: bunny      
      - family: Atkinson Hyperlegible
        source: google
    base: Atkinson Hyperlegible
    headings: 
      family: Crimson Text
      color: blue
    monospace:
      size: 0.8em
    monospace-inline: 
      color: orange
    monospace-block: 
      background-color: light-grey
format: 
  revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    theme: [default, slides.scss]
    code-line-numbers: false
---

## Getting set up

Make sure you've:

{{< qrcode https://cwickham.github.io/cascadia2026  height=300 width=300 >}}


::: footer
https://cwickham.github.io/cascadia2026
:::

## Hello, I'm Charlotte Wickham

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}

Developer Advocate  
![](images/quarto-logo-dark.svg){height="1em" style="vertical-align: middle;" fig-alt="Quarto"} @ ![](images/Posit-Logos-2024_horiz-full-color-quarto-web.svg){height="1em" style="vertical-align: middle;" fig-alt="Posit"}  

Based in Corvallis, OR
<br>



{{< iconify bi:linkedin >}} [cvwickham](https://www.linkedin.com/in/cvwickham/)  
{{< iconify bi:github >}} [cwickham](https://github.com/cwickham)  
{{< iconify bi:envelope >}} [charlotte.wickham@posit.co](mailto:charlotte.wickham@posit.co)  
:::
::: {.column width="40%"}
Workshop website
{{< qrcode https://cwickham.github.io/cascadia2026  height=300 width=300 >}}
:::
::::

::: footer
https://cwickham.github.io/cascadia2026
:::

## Your Turn

::: task
Introduce yourself to your neighbors:

-   Name
-   Location
-   What you need a website for

Check you are all [set up](#getting-set-up)
:::

{{< countdown "4:00" >}}

::: footer
https://cwickham.github.io/cascadia2026
:::

## Outline

Goal: You create a website with Quarto

1.  Don't start from scratch
3.  Add pages and customize navigation
4.  Make the style your own
5.  Publish
6.  Use listings for "lists" of content


::: footer
<https://charlotte.quarto.pub/dslc24>
:::

## What to expect

**My turns:** I'll use slides, and live code to demonstrate ideas.

**Your Turns:** You practice what you've just seen.

Ask questions!

::: footer
<https://charlotte.quarto.pub/dslc24>
:::

# 1. Don't start from scratch

## Demo: Start from a template

From a new empty project:

``` {.bash filename="Terminal"}
quarto use template cwickham/website-starter
```

## Your Turn

::: task
1. Start a new project.   
    RStudio > New Project  
    Positron > New Folder from Template > Empty Project
2. Get the template: 

    ``` {.bash filename="Terminal"}
    quarto use template cwickham/website-starter
    ```

    *Create a subdirectory for template? No*

3. Open and preview `index.qmd`.

4. Match the content in `index.qmd` to the preview.
:::

{{< countdown "5:00" >}}

## Demo: Exploring `index.qmd` {.smaller}

* Render/preview `index.qmd`. It becomes `index.html`, your homepage.

* Regular Quarto document with:
   
   - YAML Header
   - Content, possibly including code cells

* Workflow: Edit, render/preview

* Uses an `about` template: <https://quarto.org/docs/websites/website-about.html>

* Other templates: `jolla`, `trestles`, `solana`, `marquee`, or `broadside`

## Your Turn

::: {.task}

Edit `index.qmd`: 

*  Change the `title` and `subtitle`
*  Try a different template: `jolla`, `trestles`, `solana`, `marquee`, or `broadside`
*  Edit, add, or remove items in `links`. Find other `icon`s at <https://icons.getbootstrap.com/>
*  **Challenge**: Use a different `image`, and edit `image-alt`

Spare time? Edit the *content* of the page

:::

{{< countdown "8:00" >}}

## Demo: Use a different `image`

1. Move new image into project
2. Replace path
3. Edit `image-alt`

Paths in a `.qmd` are relative to the location of the `.qmd`

## What is a Quarto website?

A minimal website only needs two files:

::::: columns

:::{.column}
Configuration file:

```{.yaml filename="_quarto.yml"}
project: 
  type: website
```    
:::

:::{.column}
A homepage:

```{.markdown filename="index.qmd"}
---
title: My homepage
---
```
:::

:::::


# 2. Add pages and customize navigation

## Demo: Add a page

1.  New Quarto Document: `projects.qmd` 

    ```{.markdown shortcodes="false" filename="projects.qmd"}
    ---
    title: Projects
    ---

    Some projects I've worked on:

    ```

2.  File path becomes URL path: `{ site url }/projects.html`

## Demo: Add navigation

`_quarto.yml`: configuration file

::: {.columns}
::: {.column .fragment}
``` {.yaml filename="_quarto.yml"}
project:
  type: website
```
Identifies this as a Quarto website
:::
::: {.column .fragment}

``` {.yaml filename="_quarto.yml"}
website:
  navbar:
    right: 
      - projects.qmd
```
Controls navigation and other website settings 
:::
::: 

## More file path examples {.smaller}

File path becomes URL path

::::: columns
::: {.column width="40%"}
**File location**

`demo.qmd`

`talks/index.qmd`

`data/air-quality.csv`
:::

::: {.column width="60%"}
**URL**

`{ site url }/demo.html`

`{ site url }/talks/`

`{ site url }/data/air-quality.csv`
:::
:::::

## Your Turn 

::: {.task}

1. Add a page
2. Add a link to the page in the navigation
3. Skim <https://quarto.org/docs/websites/website-navigation.html>. **What other types of navigation are available?**

*Spare time*: Add content to the page

:::

{{< countdown "8:00" >}}

## Demo: Navigation

1. Put items on `left`
1. Switch to a `sidebar` with `content` and back again
2. Edit `tools`

<https://quarto.org/docs/websites/website-navigation.html>

<https://quarto-tdg.org/websites.html#other-navigation-elements>

# 3.  Make the style your own

## Use brand.yml: Color

``` {.yaml filename="_brand.yml"}
color:
  foreground: "#333333"
  background: "#ffffff"
  primary: "#333333"
```

::: footer
<https://quarto.org/docs/authoring/brand.html>
:::

## Use brand.yml: Fonts

``` {.yaml filename="_brand.yml"}
typography:
  fonts:
    - family: Open Sans
      source: bunny
    - family: Roboto Slab
      source: bunny
  base:
    family: Open Sans
  headings:
    family: Roboto Slab
```

`bunny`: Bunny Fonts, zero-tracking and no-logging policy <https://fonts.bunny.net>

::: footer
<https://quarto.org/docs/authoring/brand.html>
:::

## Check accessibility: `axe`

Once you start playing with colors it's easy to create contrast issues. 

Check with [`axe-core`](https://github.com/dequelabs/axe-core) by adding:

```{.yaml filename="_quarto.yml"}
format:
  html:
    axe:
      output: document
```

Remove before publishing! 

## Your Turn

::: task

1. Edit `_brand.yml` to customize colors and fonts

2. Enable the `axe` checks to check for contrast issues

:::

<https://quarto.org/docs/authoring/brand.html#brand-elements>

{{< countdown "8:00" >}}

## Use brand.yml: Code

If your pages have code, you will probably want to tweak `monospace` options:

``` {.yaml filename="_brand.yml"}
typography:
  monospace-inline:
    color: "#6b21a8"
    background-color: background 
  monospace-block:
    background-color: "#eaf2fb"
```

::: footer
<https://quarto.org/docs/authoring/brand.html>
:::

## Use brand.yml: Dark mode

Add `light` and `dark` to colors to add a dark mode toggle to your site:

``` {.yaml filename="_brand.yml"}
color:
  foreground: 
    light: "#333333"
    dark: "#ffffff"
  background: 
    light: "#ffffff"
    dark: "#333333"
  primary: 
    light: "#333333"
    dark: "#ffffff"
```

## Sometimes you might need to add SCSS  {.smaller}

:::::: columns
::: column
``` {.yaml filename="_quarto.yml"}
format:
  html:
    theme: [brand, styles.scss]
```
:::

:::: column

::: fragment
``` {.scss filename="styles.scss"}
/*-- scss:defaults --*/


/*-- scss:rules --*/

```
:::
::::
::::::


# 4. Publish

## Venues



## Demo: Use `quarto publish`

``` {.bash filename="Terminal"}
quarto publish posit-connect-cloud
```

Other venues: `gh-pages`, `netlify`, `connect`

Updates? Re-run `quarto publish`

::: footer
<https://quarto.org/docs/publishing/>
:::

## Your Turn: Posit Connect Cloud

Pick **one of**: Your Turn: Posit Connect Cloud or Your Turn: GitHub Pages

::: task

1.  Publish your site to Posit Connect Cloud:
    
    ``` {.bash filename="Terminal"}
    quarto publish posit-connect-cloud
    ```

:::

{{< countdown "10:00" >}}

## Your Turn: GitHub Pages

::: task

1.  Make your project a Git repo

2.  Add `_site/` to `.gitignore`
    
3.  Commit, and push to GitHub

4.  Publish to GitHub Pages:

    ``` {.bash filename="Terminal"}
    quarto publish gh-pages
    ```

{{< countdown "10:00" >}}
:::

# 5. Use listings for lists of content

## A listing is...

-   an automatically generated list of content
-   styled via a template, (built-in `type`, or custom `template`)
-   can be included on any page

::: footer
<https://quarto.org/docs/websites/website-listings.html>
:::

## Use listings for ...

<iframe src="https://charlotte.quarto.pub/listings/" width="100%" height="550" title="Listing Examples">

</iframe>

::: footer
<https://charlotte.quarto.pub/listings/>
:::

## `contents` can be a YAML file {.smaller}

::::: columns
::: {.column width="40%"}
``` {.yaml filename="talks/index.qmd"}
---
title: Talks
listing:
  contents: talks.yml
  type: table
  fields: [title, venue, date]
---
```
:::

::: {.column width="60%"}
``` {.yaml filename="talks/talks.yml"}
- title: "Quarto: Reproducible Publishing"
  date: 2024-03-01
  venue: Oakridge National Lab
  path: https://github.com/cwickham/oakridge
- title: What's New in Quarto?
  date: 2023-09-01
  venue: "`posit::conf(2023)`"
  path: https://youtu.be/WR08GESib9Y?si=vTxco6_yuEDV8TBC
- title: This Month I Learned...
  date: 2021-04-01
  venue: Rladies Washington D.C., via Zoom 
  path: https://cwickham.github.io/this-month-I-learned/#1
```
:::
:::::

# Other features

## Website metadata

```{.yaml filename="_quarto.yml"}
website:
  title:
  description:
  site-url:
```

## Social cards


```{.yaml filename="_quarto.yml"}
website:
  image: social-card.png
  image-alt:
  open-graph: true
```


# Wrap Up

## Quarto Websites

A minimal website only needs two files:

::::: columns

:::{.column}
```{.yaml filename="_quarto.yml"}
project: 
  type: website
```    
:::

:::{.column}
```{.markdown filename="index.qmd"}
---
title: My homepage
---
```
:::

:::::

-  Add pages by adding Quarto documents 
-  Control navigation, website settings, theming etc. in `_quarto.yml`

Explore docs at: <https://quarto.org/docs/websites/>

## Why Quarto for Websites?

-   You don't need to learn anything new for page content
-   You get nice website features out of the box: GitHub links, social cards, navigation, etc.
-   A lot of customization available via YAML options
-   Customize style as much (or as little) as you want


::: footer
<https://charlotte.quarto.pub/dslc24>
:::

## Questions?

## Post Work

*I'll post in Slack*

* Share your URL

* Ask questions in Slack

# Thank you!
